title: cystopteris elata
intro: Cystopteris elata, also known as the tall bladder fern or tall ostrich fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia, characterized by its tall stems and large, kidney-shaped fronds. Traditionally, Cystopteris elata has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe and protect the skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Cystopteris elata include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease. Cystopteris elata is often prepared as a tea or decoction, and can also be used in tincture or capsule form to harness its medicinal properties.
